title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Runtime Lens: Wall Representation
summary: One cell opens east while its neighbor stays closed west, boundary walls disappear, and rendering data becomes the maze source of truth. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the wall representation increment in reciprocal wall and passage model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.
Test round trips, adjacency, boundary ownership, positive sizes, and changed viewport dimensions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 1884 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy. A defensible solution applies Wall Representation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reciprocal wall and passage model.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_wall_cell_invariants_2(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-wall-representation-15ngx1
# Competency: maze-wall-representation.
# Case 8411: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_wall_representation_15ngx1(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_15ngx1 = "15ngx1-1xgn51-8411"
    assert evidence_variant_15ngx1
    evidence_axes_15ngx1 = (
        "grid15ngx1",
        "topology8411",
        "search1xgn51",
        "event15ngx18411",
        "access841115ngx1",
        "recovery1xgn518411",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15ngx1)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Wall Representation model · Theory Wall Representation, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Wall Representation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Wall Representation model · Theory Wall Representation, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 9142 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Wall Representation model · Theory Wall Representation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Four unrelated booleans cannot contradict each other.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-wall-representation-15ngx1.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-wall-representation-15ngx1(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_wall_representation_15ngx1\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reciprocal_openings != passage_edges)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?boundary_breaches > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?illegal_diagonals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
